requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Provide safe paths of travel to, within, and through buildings and facilities, including doorways, ramps, elevators, and pedestrian pathways such as hallways and emergency exits.
requirements.evidence: Safe paths of travel to, within, and through buildings and facilities that include but are not limited to doorways, ramps, elevators, and pedestrian pathways such as hallways and emergency exits.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Ensure safe paths of travel between facilities and public streets, pathways, sidewalks, parking areas, passenger loading and unloading zones, and bus stops.
requirements.evidence: Safe paths of travel between facilities and public streets, pathways, sidewalks, parking areas, passenger loading and unloading zones, and bus stops.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Provide access to rooms or suites within facilities, including office areas, conference facilities, dining establishments, washrooms and recreational areas.
requirements.evidence: Access to rooms or suites within facilities, including but not limited to office areas, conference facilities, dining establishments, washrooms and recreational areas, such as swimming pools, ice rinks, theatres, etc.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Ensure hotel/lodging facilities are accessible, including accommodations, restaurants, washrooms, reservation counter and conference rooms.
requirements.evidence: Hotel/lodging including but not limited to accommodations, restaurants, washrooms, reservation counter and conference rooms.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Ensure outdoor spaces such as playgrounds, picnic areas and walking trails are accessible.
requirements.evidence: Outdoor spaces including but not limited to playgrounds, picnic areas and walking trails.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: A barrier-free path of travel must be provided throughout entrance storeys and all normally occupied floor areas, including converted spaces and common corridors.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free path of travel from the entrances required... shall be provided throughout the entrance storey and within all other normally occupied floor areas.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: All pedestrian entrances to a barrier-free storey must be barrier-free and connect to a barrier-free exterior path of travel.
requirements.evidence: all pedestrian entrances to a barrier-free storey... shall be barrier-free and shall connect to a barrier-free exterior path of travel

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: If there is a vestibule, both doors shall be powered at a barrier-free entrance.
requirements.evidence: If there is a vestibule, both doors shall be powered.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: At a barrier-free entrance with more than one doorway, only one doorway is required to be designed in accordance with accessibility standards.
requirements.evidence: At a barrier-free entrance that includes more than one doorway, only one of the doorways is required to be designed in accordance with Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: If a walkway or pedestrian bridge connects two barrier-free storeys in different buildings, the path of travel must be barrier-free.
requirements.evidence: If a walkway or pedestrian bridge connects two barrier-free storeys in different buildings, the path of travel... shall be barrier-free.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Egress
requirements.description: All entrances and egress, including the primary entrance and all emergency exits, shall be designed to be barrier-free for ease of access and safety.
requirements.evidence: All entrances and egress, including the primary entrance and all emergency exits of the building, shall be designed to be barrier-free

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Signage
requirements.description: Clear and sufficient signage is required to indicate the way to, and location of, all doors designated for use by people with physical disabilities or mobility issues.
requirements.evidence: Clear and sufficient signage is required to indicate the way to, and location of, all doors designated for use by people with physical disabilities or mobility issues.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Signage must be clear and sufficient to indicate the way to, and location of, all accessible doors for people with disabilities or mobility issues.
requirements.evidence: Clear and sufficient signage is required to indicate the way to, and location of, all doors designated for use by people with physical disabilities or mobility issues.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Where escalators or inclined moving walks provide access to a floor, an interior barrier-free path of travel (e.g., elevator, lift) must also be provided to that floor.
requirements.evidence: an escalator or inclined moving walk provides access to any floor level, an interior barrier-free path of travel shall also be provided

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: A barrier-free path of travel must be provided between accessible entrances and designated parking, passenger-loading zones, and public thoroughfares.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free path of travel that complies with Subsection 3.8.3. shall be provided between a barrier-free entrance...and a designated barrier-free parking area...exterior passenger-loading zone...public thoroughfare.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Assembly occupancies and waiting rooms with fixed seats must provide a minimum number of designated wheelchair spaces as per Table 3.8.2.3.
requirements.evidence: the number of spaces designated for wheelchair use within rooms or areas with fixed seats shall conform to Table 3.8.2.3.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: At least 5% (but no more than 20) of adaptable seats in assembly occupancies must adjoin a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: At least 5% of the adaptable seats required by Sentence (5) but no more than 20 adaptable seats shall adjoin a barrier-free path of travel.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Each row of seats served by two aisles in assembly occupancies with more than 25 fixed seats must have one adaptable seat adjacent to an aisle.
requirements.evidence: each row of seats served by two aisles shall have one adaptable seat...located adjacent to one of the aisles.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: A designated companion seat for a family member or friend must be provided adjacent to each designated wheelchair space.
requirements.evidence: a designated companion seat for a family member or friend shall be provided adjacent designated wheelchair spaces.

requirements.label: Accessible Viewing Space Dimensions
requirements.description: Accessible viewing spaces in assembly areas must provide a minimum independent seating space of 850 mm by 1390 mm.
requirements.evidence: dimensions of an independent seating space within this area to be a minimum 850 by 1390 mm.

requirements.label: Maintain Required Aisle Width Behind Accessible Seating
requirements.description: Aisle width behind accessible seating areas must be maintained to ensure unobstructed passage.
requirements.evidence: the aisle width behind the accessible seating area shall be maintained

requirements.label: Minimum Headroom Clearance
requirements.description: Minimum headroom clearance in every access to exit must conform to exit requirements (e.g., 2050 mm min. as per referenced figures).
requirements.evidence: the minimum headroom clearance in every access to exit shall conform to the requirements of Article 3.4.3.4. for exits.

requirements.label: Limits of Protruding Objects
requirements.description: Protruding building elements within 1980 mm of the floor must not project more than 100 mm horizontally into paths of travel unless clearance below is less than 680 mm.
requirements.evidence: protruding building elements located within 1 980 mm of the floor shall not project more than 100 mm horizontally into paths of travel

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Barriers for Overhead Hazards
requirements.description: If overhead clearance is reduced, cane-detectable barriers (e.g., planters, railings) should be used to prevent injury.
requirements.evidence: a cane-detectable barrier (e.g., planters, fencing, benches, railings) extending within the entire associated floor area should be used for safety reasons.

requirements.label: Wheel Stops to Prevent Vehicle Intrusion
requirements.description: Wheel stops should be used to prevent vehicles from intruding onto the barrier-free path of travel on sidewalks.
requirements.evidence: wheel stops should be used to prevent vehicles from intruding onto the path of travel.

requirements.label: Curb Ramp Detectability
requirements.description: Curbs and curb ramps should be designed with colour or texture contrast to be easily detected by people with low vision, blindness, or cognitive impairments.
requirements.evidence: curb ramps...should be designed to be more easily detected by persons who have low vision, persons who are blind, or persons with cognitive impairments by colour or texture

requirements.label: Pattern and Colour Blocking for Key Access
requirements.description: Visual and tactile or textural warning systems should be provided on escalator steps and floor surfaces at the top and bottom of escalators for orientation.
requirements.evidence: careful consideration shall be given to a visual and tactile or textural warning system on escalator steps and floor surfaces at the top and bottom of the escalator.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Clear signage indicating the location of accessible washrooms, elevators, and accessible routes must be provided and be easily visible.
requirements.evidence: Clear signage indicating the location of accessible washrooms, elevators and so on should be clearly visible for ease of access.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Curb ramps for access to sidewalks or medians must be at least 760 mm wide and 1,500 mm long.
requirements.evidence: The curb ramp should be at least 760 mm wide and 1 500 mm long.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Ratios
requirements.description: Parking stalls for persons with disabilities must be provided according to Table 3.8.2.5. (e.g., 1 for 2-10 stalls, 2 for 11-25, etc.).
requirements.evidence: Table 3.8.2.5. shows the number of stalls currently required to be designated for use by persons with disabilities.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking for Adaptable Units
requirements.description: One accessible parking stall per adaptable or barrier-free dwelling unit is required.
requirements.evidence: One accessible stall per accessible or adaptable dwelling unit is required.

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Design
requirements.description: Accessible parking stalls must be designed in accordance with Article 3.8.3.23.
requirements.evidence: Parking stalls for use by persons with disabilities ... shall be designed in accordance with Article 3.8.3.23.

requirements.label: Passenger Loading Zone Accommodation
requirements.description: Passenger loading zones should accommodate side or rear loading/unloading, with a 1,500 mm × 1,500 mm area for wheelchair turning and minimum height clearance of 2,750 mm (3.2 m for specialized vehicles).
requirements.evidence: An area of 1 500 mm × 1 500 mm is required beyond the platform ... A minimum height clearance of 2 750 mm needs to be provided ... may require a minimum of 3.2 m height clearance.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel from parking areas to primary building entrance should be distinguishable with lighting, contrasting/continuous colour, texture change, and/or handrails.
requirements.evidence: The barrier-free path of travel from the parking areas to the primary building entrance should be distinguishable with the use of good lighting, contrasting and continuous colour, change in texture and/or use of handrails.

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Doors in specified locations must be equipped with power door operators allowing persons to activate opening in the intended direction of travel.
requirements.evidence: doors shall be equipped with power door operators ... that allow persons to activate the opening of the doors in the intended direction of travel

requirements.label: Universal Washroom Requirement
requirements.description: At each location where washrooms are provided on a storey requiring a barrier-free path of travel, at least one universal washroom must be provided.
requirements.evidence: at each location where washrooms are provided in a storey to which a barrier-free path of travel is required ... at least one universal washroom ... shall be provided.

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Washroom Stall Ratios
requirements.description: Where a barrier-free washroom is required, at least one accessible water-closet stall and one limited mobility stall must be provided for every 10 stalls or part thereof.
requirements.evidence: at least one accessible water-closet stall ... for every 10 stalls or part thereof ... at least one stall for persons with limited mobility ... for every 10 stalls or part thereof.

requirements.label: Accessible Lavatories and Mirrors
requirements.description: Barrier-free washrooms must have at least one lavatory and one mirror that comply with accessibility requirements.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free washroom shall be provided with a lavatory that complies ... at least one mirror shall comply ...

requirements.label: Accessible Drinking Fountains and Bottle Filling Stations
requirements.description: At each location with drinking fountains or water-bottle filling stations, at least one must comply with accessibility requirements.
requirements.evidence: at least one of them shall comply with Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Accessible Showers and Bathtubs
requirements.description: Where showers or bathtubs are provided, at least one must comply with accessibility requirements, except in specified occupancies.
requirements.evidence: at least one shower stall shall comply ... Where a bathtub is installed ... it shall comply ...

requirements.label: Universal Dressing and Shower Room
requirements.description: Where a showering facility is provided for public/customers or as a common-use area for employees, at least one universal dressing and shower room must be provided.
requirements.evidence: at least one universal dressing and shower room conforming to Subsection 3.8.3. shall be provided.

requirements.label: Accessible Change Space in Large Occupancies
requirements.description: In certain large occupancies (>500), at least one universal washroom on the main barrier-free entrance storey must have an accessible change space.
requirements.evidence: at least one universal washroom ... shall incorporate an accessible change space conforming to Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Assistive listening systems must be provided in assembly occupancy buildings in all classrooms, auditoria, meeting rooms, and theatres over 100 m2.
requirements.evidence: all classrooms, auditoria, meeting rooms and theatres with an area of more than 100 m2 shall be equipped with an assistive listening system

requirements.label: Assistive Listening at Service Counters
requirements.description: At least one service counter in assembly occupancy buildings must have an assistive listening system or adaptive technology, and amplification if there is a communication barrier.
requirements.evidence: at least one of the service counters shall be equipped with ... an assistive listening system or adaptive technology ... and ... an amplification system, where there is a barrier to communication

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Signs providing visual information must be installed to indicate the location of barrier-free entrances, washrooms, showers, elevators, parking spaces, and assistive listening systems.
requirements.evidence: Signs providing visual information ... shall be installed to indicate the location of ... barrier-free entrances, ... washrooms, ... showers, ... elevators, ... parking spaces, and ... assistive listening systems

requirements.label: Braille, Visual, and Audio Cues
requirements.description: Where a washroom is not accessible, signs with visual and tactile information must indicate the location of barrier-free facilities.
requirements.evidence: signs providing visual and tactile information ... shall be installed to indicate the location of barrier-free facilities.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel must have a clear width of at least 1,000 mm, with specific exceptions for short reduced-width sections and required turning spaces.
requirements.evidence: the clear width of a barrier-free path of travel shall be not less than 1 000 mm.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel over 24 m must have a passing space of at least 1,700 mm wide and 1,700 mm long at intervals not exceeding 24 m.
requirements.evidence: The width of a barrier-free path of travel that is more than 24 m long shall be increased to not less than 1 700 mm for a length of 1 700 mm at intervals not exceeding 24 m.

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Where a section of a barrier-free path is less than 1,500 mm wide for more than 12 m, it must end in a clear floor space of specified dimensions.
requirements.evidence: Where a section of a barrier-free path of travel is less than 1 500 mm wide for a distance of more than 12 m, it shall end in a clear floor space that is not less than 1 700 mm in diameter...

values.clear_space: 1700 mm diameter or 1700 mm x 1500 mm or T-shaped 1700 mm x 1500 mm

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Interior and exterior walking surfaces in barrier-free paths must be stable, firm, slip-resistant, and have no openings larger than 13 mm.
requirements.evidence: be stable, firm and slip-resistant, have no opening that will permit the passage of a sphere more than 13 mm in diameter

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Barrier-free path of travel may include ramps, elevators, or platform-equipped devices to overcome level changes.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free path of travel is permitted to include ramps, passenger elevators or other platform-equipped passenger-elevating devices to overcome a difference in level.

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Changes in level greater than 13 mm in a barrier-free path must be provided with sloped floors or ramps.
requirements.evidence: be provided with sloped floors or ramps at changes in level more than 13 mm.

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Elongated openings in walking surfaces must be oriented approximately perpendicular to the direction of travel.
requirements.evidence: have any elongated openings oriented approximately perpendicular to the direction of travel

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Exterior walks forming part of a barrier-free path must have a slip-resistant, continuous, and even surface.
requirements.evidence: Exterior walks that form part of a barrier-free path of travel shall have a slip-resistant, continuous and even surface

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Level area must be provided adjacent to an entrance doorway on exterior walks forming part of a barrier-free path.
requirements.evidence: have a level area conforming to Clause 3.8.3.5.(1)(c) adjacent to an entrance doorway

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Where a service counter is provided, at least one section must comply with accessibility requirements.
requirements.evidence: Where a service counter is provided, at least one section of it shall comply with Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Accessible Public Telephones
requirements.description: In each location with public telephones, at least one must comply with accessibility requirements.
requirements.evidence: at least one telephone shall comply with Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Downward changes in elevation in a barrier-free path must be signaled by tactile attention indicator surfaces per CSA B651.
requirements.evidence: a downward change in elevation shall be signaled by the use of a tactile attention indicator surface complying with Clauses 4.3.5.3.1., 4.3.5.3.3. and 4.3.5.3.4. of CSA B651

requirements.label: Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicators must be installed at the top of unenclosed stairs and at drop-off edges with elevation change >300 mm unprotected by a guard.
requirements.evidence: tactile attention indicators...shall be installed at the top of flights of stairs that are unenclosed, and at drop-off edges with a change in elevation greater than 300 mm that are unprotected by a guard.

requirements.label: Tactile Attention Indicator Configuration
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicator surfaces must use truncated domes 4–5 mm high, top diameter 12–25 mm, base diameter 10±1 mm greater, in a square grid, with specified spacing.
requirements.evidence: A tactile attention indicator surface shall be composed of truncated domes: a. with a height of between 4 and 5 mm; b. with the top diameter between 12 and 25 mm and the base diameter 10 ± 1 mm greater than the top diameter; c. arranged in a square grid; and d. with a centre-to-centre distance of adjacent domes complying with Table 5

requirements.label: Tactile Attention Indicator Installation
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicator surfaces must be installed along the full width of the hazard, 600–650 mm deep, with one side against the edge of the hazard.
requirements.evidence: it shall be installed along the full width of the hazard: a. to a depth between 600 and 650 mm; and b. with one side against the edge of the hazard

requirements.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
requirements.description: Tactile attention indicator surfaces must have a luminance (colour) contrast of at least 50% with adjacent surfaces using the Michelson contrast formula.
requirements.evidence: it shall have a luminance (colour) contrast of at least 50% with the adjacent surface using the Michelson contrast formula

requirements.label: Tactile Direction Indicator Configuration
requirements.description: Tactile direction indicator surfaces must use flat-topped, parallel, elongated bars 4–5 mm high, top width 17–30 mm, base width top+10±1 mm, top length ≥270 mm.
requirements.evidence: A tactile direction indicator surface shall be composed of flat- topped, parallel, elongated bars having: a. a height of 4 to 5 mm; b. a top width between 17 and 30 mm and a base width 10 ± 1 mm greater than the top width; ... d. a top length not less than 270 mm

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Exterior walks and public access routes must be designed to meet the needs of seniors and persons with disabilities, providing a safe, continuous, and uninterrupted barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: All designated public access routes should be designed to meet the needs of seniors and persons with disabilities. The surface of a walkway must have a hard and even surface that will permit a safe, continuous, and uninterrupted barrier-free path of travel.

values.min_width: 1600 mm (recommended 1800 mm for two deaf people side by side)

requirements.label: Ramps
requirements.description: Ramps in barrier-free paths must have a clear width of at least 1000 mm, a maximum slope of 1:12, and level areas at top, bottom, and turns. Handrails and guards are required.
requirements.evidence: A ramp located in a barrier-free path of travel shall a. have a clear width not less than 1 000 mm... b. have a uniform slope along its length not more than 1 in 12...

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Curb ramps and blended transitions must comply with CSA B651, with specified running and cross slopes, tactile attention indicators, and turning spaces.
requirements.evidence: Curb ramps within a barrier-free path of travel shall comply with Clause 8.3.3. of CSA B651... The running slope at a curb ramp or blended transition shall be... for a curb ramp, between a ratio of 1:15 (6.66.%) and 1:10 (10%)...

requirements.label: Non-Slip Flooring
requirements.description: Surfaces of walkways, ramps, and landings must be hard, even, and slip-resistant. Pre-cast units should be flush and level; joints must not exceed 13 mm width and 6 mm depth.
requirements.evidence: the selected material for the surface must be slip-resistant... Joints in sidewalks or between bricks or stones shall not exceed 13 mm in width and 6 mm in depth and shall be flush with the adjoining surfaces.

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Pathways across parking lots and large plazas can be identified by the use of different textures and contrasting colours to assist persons with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: To assist persons with visual disabilities, the surface of the walk should be easily discernible from the surrounding areas. Pathways across parking lots and large plazas can be identified by the use of different textures and contrasting colours.

requirements.label: Overhead Obstruction Clearance
requirements.description: Overhead objects along walkways must be at least 1980 mm high to avoid becoming a hazard for persons with visual impairments.
requirements.evidence: Overhead objects (e.g., signage, tree branches/limbs, free-standing staircases/escalators) should be at a height or 1 980 mm or greater to avoid becoming a hazard.

requirements.label: Passenger Loading Zone Accessibility
requirements.description: Exterior passenger-loading zones must have an access aisle at least 1500 mm wide and 6000 mm long, a curb ramp if needed, and a clear height of at least 2750 mm.
requirements.evidence: If an exterior passenger-loading zone is provided, it shall have a. an access aisle not less than 1 500 mm wide and 6 000 mm long... c. a clear height of not less than 2 750 mm...

requirements.label: Rest Area Widening
requirements.description: At intervals not exceeding every 9 m, walkways should be widened to a minimum of 2000 mm for a length of 2000 mm to act as a rest area.
requirements.evidence: it is recommended that at intervals not exceeding every 9 m, the walkway should be widened to a minimum of 2 000 mm for a length of 2 000 mm to act as a rest area

requirements.label: Edge Protection for Ramps
requirements.description: Ramps and landings not at grade or adjacent to a wall must have edge protection: a curb at least 75 mm high or a raised barrier/rail not more than 100 mm from the surface.
requirements.evidence: Ramps and landings not at grade or adjacent to a wall shall have edge protection consisting of a. a curb not less than 75 mm high, or b. a raised barrier or rail located not more than 100 mm from the ramp or landing surface.

requirements.label: Grating Openings in Walkways
requirements.description: Gratings in walkways must have a maximum clear opening of 13 mm.
requirements.evidence: If these covers must be in walkways, the gratings shall have a maximum clear opening of 13 mm.

requirements.label: Tactile Warning Indicators
requirements.description: Tactile warning indicators are required for ease of cane detection and safety at curb ramps and other key locations.
requirements.evidence: Reminder: Tactile warning indicators are required for ease of cane detection and safety.

requirements.label: Level Area at Entrances
requirements.description: A level area of at least 1500 mm by 1500 mm is to be provided in front of the entrance to allow ease of access into or out of a building.
requirements.evidence: A level area of at least 1 500 mm by 1 500 mm is to be provided in front of the entrance to allow ease of access into or out of a building.

requirements.label: Obstruction-Free Area
requirements.description: Areas free from obstructions (e.g., directional signs, tree branches, guy wires) are required to aid persons with visual impairments and mobility aid users.
requirements.evidence: The requirement to have an area free from obstructions is primarily to aid persons with visual impairments and persons who are blind or elderly who may require the use of a mobility aid.

requirements.label: Colour Contrast on Ramps and Landings
requirements.description: Ramps, landings, and treads accessible to the public must have either a colour contrast or a distinctive pattern to demarcate the leading edge and the beginning/end of a ramp.
requirements.evidence: shall have either a colour contrast or a distinctive pattern to demarcate the leading edge of the tread and the leading edge of the landing, as well as the beginning and end of a ramp.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Exterior exit stairs over 10 m high must be designed to prevent ice and snow accumulation.
requirements.evidence: Treads and landings of exterior exit stairs more than 10 m high shall be designed to be free of ice and snow accumulations.

requirements.label: Ramp Edge Protection and Visual Contrast
requirements.description: Ramps should have curbs, handrails, and edge protection with contrasting colours for safety.
requirements.evidence: Curbs can be of contrasting colour from the ramp and handrails can be a contrasting colour from the adjacent surface as best practice for edge protection at the sides of ramps.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Doorways in barrier-free paths must have a clear width of at least 850 mm when open.
requirements.evidence: every doorway that is located in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a clear width not less than 850 mm when the door is in the open position.

values.text: clear width not less than 850 mm

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Power door operators must activate automatically or by accessible controls, be marked, and be operable at specified heights.
requirements.evidence: Power door operators required by Sentences 3.8.2.7.(1) and 3.8.5.2.(2) shall activate automatically or through the use of controls that ... are operable from a height between 150 mm and 300 mm as well as between 900 mm and 1 100 mm above the floor.

values.text: controls operable at 150-300 mm and 900-1100 mm above floor

requirements.label: Threshold Height and Bevel
requirements.description: Door thresholds in barrier-free paths must not exceed 13 mm in height and must be beveled for wheelchair passage.
requirements.evidence: A threshold for a doorway referred to in Sentence (2) and (3) shall be not more than 13 mm higher than the finished floor surface and shall be beveled to facilitate the passage of wheelchairs.

values.text: threshold not more than 13 mm high

requirements.label: Cane-Detectable Guard for Power Doors
requirements.description: Install cane-detectable guards on the hinged side of power-assisted doors that swing into the path of travel.
requirements.evidence: A cane-detectable guard shall be installed on the hinged side of power-assisted doors that swing open into the path of travel.

requirements.label: Door Opening Force
requirements.description: Doors in barrier-free paths must open with a force not exceeding 38 N (exterior) or 22 N (interior/sliding).
requirements.evidence: a door in a barrier-free path of travel shall open when the force applied ... is not more than 38 N in the case of an exterior swinging door, 22 N in the case of an interior swinging door, or 22 N in the case of a sliding door.

requirements.label: Door Closer Timing
requirements.description: Interior doors in barrier-free paths must have a closing period of at least 3 seconds from 70° open to 75 mm from closed.
requirements.evidence: a closer for an interior door in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a closing period of not less than 3 s measured from when the door is in an open position of 70° to the doorway, to when the door reaches a point 75 mm from the closed position

values.text: closing period not less than 3 s

requirements.label: Clear Space Beside Doors
requirements.description: Swinging doors in barrier-free paths must have clear space on the latch side: 600 mm (toward approach), 300 mm (away from approach).
requirements.evidence: a swinging door in a barrier-free path of travel shall have a clear space on the latch side ... not less than 600 mm beyond the edge of the door opening if the door swings toward the approach side, and 300 mm ... if the door swings away

requirements.label: Vestibule Space for Wheelchairs
requirements.description: Vestibules in barrier-free paths must allow wheelchair movement and provide at least 1,350 mm plus door width between doors in series.
requirements.evidence: a vestibule located in a barrier-free path of travel shall ... provide a distance between 2 doors in series of not less than 1 350 mm plus the width of any door that swings into the space

requirements.label: Clear Floor Space at Doors (Pull Side)
requirements.description: Clear floor space on the pull side of swinging doors in barrier-free paths must be at least 1,700 mm by 1,500 mm.
requirements.evidence: the clear floor space on the pull side of a swinging door in a barrier-free path of travel shall be level within a rectangular area of not less than 1 700 mm by 1 500 mm

requirements.label: Clear Floor Space at Doors (Push/Sliding Side)
requirements.description: Clear floor space on the push side of swinging doors and both sides of sliding doors in barrier-free paths must be at least 1,200 mm by 1,500 mm.
requirements.evidence: the clear floor space on the push side of a swinging door and on each side of a sliding door in a barrier-free path of travel shall be level within a rectangular area ... not less than 1 200 mm ... not less than 1 500 mm

requirements.label: Reduced Clear Floor Space for Power Doors
requirements.description: Where equipped with a power door operator, clear floor space parallel to the closed door may be reduced to 1,000 mm.
requirements.evidence: where a door ... is equipped with a power door operator ... the width of the clear floor space parallel to the closed door is permitted to be reduced to not less than 1 000 mm.

requirements.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
requirements.description: Door or door frame leading from public corridors to public areas or exits must have readily apparent visual contrast with adjacent wall surfaces.
requirements.evidence: doorways ... shall be provided with a door or door frame that has a readily apparent visual contrast with adjacent wall surfaces.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Doors in a barrier-free path of travel must provide minimum clear widths depending on door location and type (e.g., ≥ 850 mm or ≥ 1 000 mm).
requirements.evidence: providing a clear width not less than 850 mm in the open position if located on the short side... or not less than 1 000 mm... on the long side

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Power-operated doors must have battery backup or be manually operable in the event of a power failure.
requirements.evidence: In the event of a power failure, power-operated doors must have battery back-up or be manually operable.

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Transparent doors and panels must have visually contrasting hardware, bars, or markings to make their existence and position readily apparent.
requirements.evidence: a glass or transparent door shall be designed and constructed so that the existence and position of the door is readily apparent, by attaching visually contrasting hardware, bars or other permanent fixtures

requirements.label: Accessible Door Handles
requirements.description: Lever handles with ends turned toward the door are recommended over knob-type handles for ease of use and safety for people with limited strength or visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Knob-type handles are not recommended... Lever handles with the ends turned toward the door are less of a hazard

requirements.label: Clear Floor Space at Doors
requirements.description: Minimum clear floor space must be provided at doors in a barrier-free path of travel, with specific dimensions depending on door type and operator.
requirements.evidence: Minimum clear floor space required at doors in a barrier-free path of travel

values.text: ≥ 1 500 mm, ≥ 1 700 mm, ≥ 1 000 mm, etc. (see figure for details)

requirements.label: Elevator Car Size for Stretchers
requirements.description: At least one elevator must accommodate a patient stretcher 2 010 mm long and 610 mm wide, with additional space for attendants.
requirements.evidence: at least one elevator... shall have inside dimensions that will accommodate and provide adequate access for a patient stretcher 2 010 mm long and 610 mm wide

values.text: 2 010 mm x 610 mm minimum clear space

requirements.label: Elevator Car Door Width
requirements.description: Elevator access doors must have a minimum width of 1 067 mm (on 2 032 mm side) or 915 mm (on 1 295 mm side).
requirements.evidence: minimum access door width is 1 067 mm... or minimum 915 mm wide access door

requirements.label: Elevator Weight Capacity
requirements.description: Passenger elevators must have a minimum weight capacity of 6 600 kg; platform lifts must have a minimum of 4 400 kg.
requirements.evidence: passenger elevators (minimum 6 600 kg weight capacity), platform lifts (minimum 4 400 kg weight capacity)

requirements.label: Elevator Operation in Barrier-Free Path
requirements.description: Elevators should be operational in buildings in relation to barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: Elevators should be operational in buildings in relation to barrier-free path of travel.

requirements.label: Elevator/Lift Code Compliance
requirements.description: Elevators and lifts must comply with the latest Elevating Devices Codes Regulation, including ASME A17.1/CSA B44 and CSA-B355.
requirements.evidence: Reference the latest version of the Elevating Devices Codes Regulation... ASME A17.1-2022/CSA-B44:22... CSA-B355:19

requirements.label: Glazing Safety Standards
requirements.description: Glass doors must use laminated or tempered safety glazing conforming to CAN/CGSB-12.1 or wired glass conforming to CAN/CGSB-12.11-M.
requirements.evidence: constructed of laminated or tempered safety glazing conforming to CAN/CGSB-12.1... or wired glass conforming to CAN/CGSB-12.11-M

requirements.label: Barrier for Low Windows in Public Areas
requirements.description: Windows in public areas extending below 1 000 mm above the floor and above the second storey must be protected by a barrier or be non-openable.
requirements.evidence: A window in a public area that extends to less than 1 000 mm above the floor... shall be protected by a barrier or railing

requirements.label: Accessible Directory at Entrances
requirements.description: Entrance directories must be visible from a seated position to accommodate visitors with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Ensure that the directory is visible from a seated position.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Entrances with security locks must provide both an audible and visual signal to alert visitors when the locking mechanism is deactivated.
requirements.evidence: Barrier-free design requires that both an audible and visual signal be provided to alert visitors that the locking mechanism is deactivated.

requirements.label: Visual Contrast Between Walls and Floors
requirements.description: Doorways must be identified by contrasting colours for door frames, handles, awnings, etc.
requirements.evidence: Doorways shall be identified by the use of contrasting colours for door frames, handles, awnings, etc.

requirements.label: Lighting at Entrances
requirements.description: Entrances and vestibules must be well lit with evenly distributed and high levels of lighting.
requirements.evidence: Entrances and vestibules should be well lit. Additionally, entrances... shall be illuminated with evenly distributed and high levels of lighting.

requirements.label: Audible and Visible Feedback for Controls
requirements.description: Where controls provide a feedback signal, it must be both audible and visible.
requirements.evidence: where controls provide a feedback signal to the user, it shall be both audible and visible

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Visual information signs must comply with CSA B651 standards for accessible design.
requirements.evidence: Visual information signs... shall comply with Clauses 4.5.2, 4.5.3 and 4.5.4 of CSA B651

requirements.label: Braille, Visual, and Audio Cues
requirements.description: Tactile information signs must have Braille and tactile characters per CSA B651, and be installed at specified locations and heights.
requirements.evidence: Tactile information signs... shall have Braille and tactile characters in accordance with Clauses 4.5.6.2 and 4.5.6.3 of CSA B651

values.text: Braille/tactile, 1 500 mm above floor, ≤ 300 mm from door

requirements.label: Accessible Control Mounting Height
requirements.description: Controls must be mounted 900 mm to 1 200 mm above the floor and adjacent to a clear floor space of 1 350 mm by 800 mm.
requirements.evidence: be mounted 900 mm to 1 200 mm above the floor... centred on either the length or the width of a clear floor space of 1 350 mm by 800 mm

values.text: 900–1 200 mm height; 1 350 mm x 800 mm clear space

requirements.label: Accessible Control Operation
requirements.description: Controls must be operable with one hand in a closed fist position, without tight grasping, pinching, or twisting, and require no more than 22 N of force.
requirements.evidence: be operable with one hand in a closed fist position, without requiring tight grasping, pinching with fingers, or twisting of the wrist... with a force not more than 22 N

requirements.label: Non-Text Diagrams and Symbols
requirements.description: Signs must incorporate the International Symbol of Access or the International Symbol of Access for Hearing Loss and appropriate graphical or textual information.
requirements.evidence: shall incorporate the International Symbol of Access or the International Symbol of Access for Hearing Loss and appropriate graphical or textual information

requirements.label: Braille, Visual, and Audio Cues
requirements.description: Building directories should be tactile, on a sloping plane 760–900 mm above the floor, with characters raised at least 0.7 mm.
requirements.evidence: Building directories should be conveniently located, tactile in nature, and located on a sloping plane 760 mm to 900 mm above the floor. Characters that are raised at least 0.7 mm are needed.

requirements.label: Haptic and Tactile Maps
requirements.description: Directions for blind persons can use pre-recorded messages or tactile maps and signs.
requirements.evidence: Persons who are blind can be given directions by the use of pre-recorded messages or the use of tactile maps and signs.

requirements.label: Pattern and Colour Blocking for Key Access
requirements.description: Orientation cues for visually disabled users include colour contrast/brightness, changes in illumination, sounds, textures, patterns, and placement of elements.
requirements.evidence: rely on the following cues for orientation: • colour contrast/brightness • changes in illumination • sounds • textures ... • patterns • placement of elements

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Use graphics or internationally recognized symbols to indicate direction or identify facilities and services for people with language or cognitive difficulties.
requirements.evidence: The use of graphics or internationally recognized symbols to indicate direction or identify facilities and services will be beneficial to persons with: • developmental disabilities, ... • little or no knowledge of the local written language.

requirements.label: Simple and Concise Language on Signs
requirements.description: If wording is used on signs, the language should be simple and concise to be accessible for most building users.
requirements.evidence: if wording is to be used on signs, the language on them should be simple and concise to be accessible for most building users.

requirements.label: High Colour Contrast for Orientation Signs
requirements.description: Identification of floors and orientation signs for visually disabled users should offer maximum colour contrast, preferably white on black or black on white.
requirements.evidence: identification of floors and other signs intended to help orient people who are visually disabled should offer maximum colour contrast ... white on black or black on white

requirements.label: Glare-Free Sign Surfaces
requirements.description: Sign surfaces should be processed to prevent glare; assigned floor numbers should have a glare-free surface and be mounted on a contrasting background.
requirements.evidence: sign surfaces be processed to prevent glare. Assigned floor numbers should have a glare-free surface and be mounted on a background in a contrasting colour.

requirements.label: Tactile Signs for Doors and Openings
requirements.description: Doors and openings from public places should be identified by tactile signs with letters ≥60 mm high, raised 0.7 mm, located 1200 mm above floor, ≤150 mm from door.
requirements.evidence: should be identified by specific tactile signs with letters not less than 60 mm high, raised 0.7 mm above the surface located 1 200 mm above the floor surface, and beginning not more than 150 mm from the door openings.

requirements.label: Lighting, Audible, Tactile, Colour/Contrast, and Ergonomic Cues
requirements.description: Orientation for visually disabled persons should use lighting, audible, tactile, colour/contrast, and ergonomic cues for wayfinding and safety.
requirements.evidence: Persons with visual disabilities rely on • lighting cues ... • audible cues ... • tactile cues ... • colour/contrast cues ... • ergonomic cues

requirements.label: Infrared Wireless Communication for Wayfinding
requirements.description: Install infrared wireless communication systems (e.g., Talking Signs) to provide remote directional voice messages for visually impaired users.
requirements.evidence: infrared wireless communication systems, where pedestrians who are visually impaired may use a receiver to pick up remote directional voice messages.

requirements.label: Physical Accessibility
requirements.description: Drinking fountains and water-bottle filling stations must be on barrier-free paths, have clear floor space (800 mm x 1350 mm), and accessible controls.
requirements.evidence: Drinking fountains ... shall a. be located along the barrier-free path of travel, b. have a minimum clear floor space of 800 mm by 1 350 mm in front of them, ... e. be equipped with controls that i. activate automatically, or ii. comply with Clause 3.8.3.8.(1)(b)

requirements.label: Accessible Water-Closet Stall Dimensions and Features
requirements.description: Accessible stalls must allow 1500 mm turning diameter, 1500 mm x 900 mm lateral transfer space, 1700 mm x 1700 mm clear floor space, 850 mm door opening, and specific grab bar and fixture placements.
requirements.evidence: Water-closet stalls ... a. be designed to allow a person using a wheelchair to turn in an open space that has a diameter not less than 1 500 mm, b. ... clear lateral transfer space ... at least 1 500 mm long ... at least 900 mm wide ... c. ... clear floor space of 1 700 mm by 1 700 mm in front of the accessible stall, ... d. ... clear opening not less than 850 mm wide ...

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Universal washrooms must be served by a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: A universal washroom shall a. be served by a barrier-free path of travel

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: Universal washrooms require a power-operated door along a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: A universal washroom requires a power-operated door since it is along a barrier-free path of travel.

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Universal washrooms must have a shelf not more than 1,200 mm above the floor unless a counter is provided.
requirements.evidence: unless a counter is provided, have a shelf located not more than 1 200 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Universal washrooms must permit a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1,700 mm in diameter.
requirements.evidence: be designed to permit a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1 700 mm in diameter.

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Universal washrooms must have a clear lateral transfer space adjacent to the water closet conforming to specified dimensions.
requirements.evidence: have a clear lateral transfer space adjacent to the water closet that conforms to Clause 3.8.3.12.(1)(b)

requirements.label: Grab Bar Specifications
requirements.description: Grab bars must be slip-resistant, free of sharp elements, 30-40 mm in diameter, resist 1.3 kN load, and have 35-45 mm wall clearance.
requirements.evidence: Grab bars shall a. be slip-resistant... d. be 30 mm to 40 mm in diameter, and e. ...clearance of 35 mm to 45 mm from the wall.

requirements.label: Adult Change Table Space
requirements.description: Universal washrooms with accessible change space must have an adult-sized change table and clear floor space 810 mm x 1,830 mm, not overlapping other required clear spaces.
requirements.evidence: have a clear floor space to accommodate the adult-sized change table that is 810 mm wide by 1 830 mm long and does not overlap with the clear spaces

requirements.label: Change Table Transfer Space
requirements.description: A clear transfer space of 900 mm by 1,350 mm must be provided adjacent to the long side of the adult-sized change table.
requirements.evidence: have a clear transfer space of 900 mm by 1 350 mm adjacent to the long side of the clear floor space for the adult-sized change table.

requirements.label: Water Closet Height and Controls
requirements.description: Water closet seat height must be 430-460 mm above floor; flush control 500-900 mm above floor, no more than 350 mm from transfer side.
requirements.evidence: be equipped with a seat located 430 mm to 460 mm above the floor...flushing control that i. is located 500 mm to 900 mm above the floor, ii. is located no more than 350 mm from the transfer side

requirements.label: Limited Mobility Stall Dimensions
requirements.description: Water-closet stalls for limited mobility must be at least 1,500 mm deep and 890-940 mm wide, with a door clear opening of at least 850 mm.
requirements.evidence: be at least 1 500 mm deep and 890 mm to 940 mm wide...provides a clear opening not less than 850 mm wide

requirements.label: Limited Mobility Stall Grab Bars
requirements.description: Horizontal grab bars on each side of the water closet, 750-850 mm above floor, begin ≤300 mm from wall behind, extend ≥450 mm in front of seat.
requirements.evidence: horizontal grab bar...i. is located 750 mm to 850 mm above the floor, ii. begins not more than 300 mm from the wall behind...iii. extends at least 450 mm in front of the toilet seat

requirements.label: Urinal Accessibility
requirements.description: Urinals must be wall-mounted, rim ≤430 mm above floor, clear approach 800 mm x 1,350 mm, no step, flush control automatic or 900-1,100 mm above floor.
requirements.evidence: be wall-mounted, with the rim located not more than 430 mm above the floor...clear width of approach...at least 800 mm wide by 1 350 mm long...no step in front...flush control...automatic, or...900 mm to 1 100 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Urinal Grab Bars
requirements.description: Vertically mounted grab bar on each side of urinal, not less than 600 mm long, centre line 1,000 mm above floor, located ≤380 mm from urinal centre.
requirements.evidence: grab bar...not less than 600 mm long, with its centre line 1 000 mm above the floor, and...not more than 380 mm from the centre line of the urinal.

requirements.label: Lavatory Clear Floor Space
requirements.description: Clear floor space in front of lavatory must be at least 800 mm wide (centred) and 1,350 mm long, with ≤430 mm beneath lavatory.
requirements.evidence: have a clear floor space in front of the lavatory that is at least i. 800 mm wide, centred on the lavatory, and ii. 1 350 mm long, of which no more than 430 mm is beneath the lavatory

requirements.label: Lavatory Rim and Clearance
requirements.description: Lavatory rim height ≤865 mm above floor; clearance beneath lavatory: 800 mm wide, 735 mm high at front, 685 mm high at 200 mm back, 230 mm high at 280-430 mm back.
requirements.evidence: have a rim height not more than 865 mm above the floor...clearance beneath...800 mm wide...735 mm high at the front edge...685 mm high at a point 200 mm back...230 mm high over...280 mm to...430 mm back

requirements.label: Lavatory Dispensers
requirements.description: Soap dispenser automatic or ≤1,100 mm above floor, within 500 mm from front of lavatory; towel dispenser/hand-drying equipment ≤1,200 mm above floor, close to lavatory.
requirements.evidence: soap dispenser...automatic, or...not more than 1 100 mm above the floor, within 500 mm from the front of the lavatory...towel dispenser...not more than 1 200 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Mirror Mounting
requirements.description: Mirrors must be mounted with bottom edge ≤1,000 mm above floor or fixed in an inclined position usable by a person in a wheelchair.
requirements.evidence: Mirrors...shall be a. mounted with their bottom edge not more than 1 000 mm above the floor, or b. fixed in an inclined position so as to be usable by a person in a wheelchair.

requirements.label: Lavatory Faucet Controls
requirements.description: Faucets must operate automatically or have manual controls not requiring continuous force, and provide at least 10 seconds of water flow if metered.
requirements.evidence: faucets...a. operate automatically, or b. have a manual control that...does not require the application of continuous force...provides at least 10 s of water flow.

requirements.label: Shower Dimensions and Threshold
requirements.description: Showers must be ≥1,500 mm wide and 900 mm deep, clear floor space at entrance ≥900 mm deep, threshold ≤13 mm high (if >6 mm, beveled ≤1:2 slope).
requirements.evidence: Showers...a. be not less than 1 500 mm wide and 900 mm deep...b. have a clear floor space at the entrance...not less than 900 mm deep...e. have a threshold not more than 13 mm higher than the finished floor, and where it is higher than 6 mm, beveled to a slope no steeper than 1 in 2 (50%)

requirements.label: Shower Grab Bars
requirements.description: Showers must have 2 grab bars conforming to specifications, one vertical, ≥1,000 mm long, 50-80 mm from clear floor space, lower end 600-650 mm above floor.
requirements.evidence: have 2 grab bars i. that conform to Sentence 3.7.2.7.(1), ii. one of which is not less than 1 000 mm long and located vertically on the side wall 50 mm to 80 mm from the adjacent clear floor space, with its lower end 600 mm to 650 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: Universal dressing and shower rooms must be located in a barrier-free path of travel.
requirements.evidence: be located in a barrier-free path of travel

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Barrier-free service counters must be at least 800 mm long, max 865 mm high, with knee space min 800 mm wide, 685 mm high, 485 mm deep.
requirements.evidence: A section of a service counter required to be barrier-free...not less than 800 mm long...surface not more than 865 mm above the floor...knee space...not less than 800 mm wide...685 mm high...485 mm deep.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Spaces for wheelchair users in assembly occupancies must be at least 900 mm wide and 1700 mm long (side approach) or 1350 mm long (front/rear approach).
requirements.evidence: they shall be not less than 900 mm wide and 1 700 mm long to allow a person using a wheelchair to enter from a side approach and 1 350 mm long where the person using a wheelchair enters from the front or rear of the space

requirements.label: Glazing Visual Indication
requirements.description: Parking stalls for persons using a wheelchair or mobility aid must be clearly marked and identified by a vertically mounted sign and the International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement.
requirements.evidence: be clearly marked and identified by...a vertically mounted sign...and...the International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement

requirements.label: Accessible Shower Threshold
requirements.description: Accessible showers should be curb less or have a threshold not exceeding 6 mm, beveled to a slope no steeper than 1 in 4.
requirements.evidence: threshold...should not exceed 6 mm for safety and shall be beveled to a slope no steeper than 1 in 4

requirements.label: Shower Seat Requirements
requirements.description: Accessible showers must have a hinged or fixed seat, not less than 450 mm wide and 400 mm deep, mounted 460–480 mm above the floor, supporting at least 1.3 kN.
requirements.evidence: have a hinged seat...not less than 450 mm wide and 400 mm deep...mounted...460 mm to 480 mm above the floor...designed to carry a minimum load of 1.3 kN

requirements.label: Shower Controls Accessibility
requirements.description: Shower controls must be mounted on the wall opposite the entrance, not more than 1200 mm above the floor, and within reach of the seat.
requirements.evidence: controls...mounted on the wall opposite the entrance to the shower at not more than 1 200 mm above the floor and within reach of the seat

requirements.label: Hand-Held Shower Head Accessibility
requirements.description: Hand-held shower head must have at least 1800 mm of flexible hose, be reachable from a seated position, and usable at 1200 mm and 2030 mm heights.
requirements.evidence: hand-held shower head with not less than 1 800 mm of flexible hose...can be used in a fixed position at a height of 1 200 mm and 2 030 mm

requirements.label: Recessed Soap Holder Accessibility
requirements.description: Recessed soap holders in showers must be reachable from the seated position and located on the side wall 100–200 mm in front of the seat.
requirements.evidence: recessed soap holders that can be reached from the seated position and located on the side wall between 100 mm and 200 mm in front of the seat

requirements.label: Universal Dressing and Shower Room Bench
requirements.description: Universal dressing and shower rooms must have a bench at least 1830 mm long, 760 mm wide, 480–520 mm high, with a 900 mm wide clear transfer space.
requirements.evidence: bench that is at least 1 830 mm long by 760 mm wide and 480 mm to 520 mm high...clear transfer space...900 mm wide and as long as the bench

requirements.label: Adult Change Table Dimensions
requirements.description: Adult change tables must be 760 mm by 1830 mm, height adjustable from 450–500 mm (low) to 850–900 mm (high), with a 900 mm by 1830 mm clear floor space in front.
requirements.evidence: change bench is 760 by 1830 mm, with a surface height...450 and 500 mm at the low range to...850 and 900 mm at the high range...minimum clear floor space 900 by 1830 mm in front

requirements.label: Bathtub Accessibility Requirements
requirements.description: Accessible bathtubs must have a clear floor space at least 1700 mm diameter, be at least 1500 mm long, with 900 mm wide clear space along its length, and accessible controls.
requirements.evidence: room with a clear floor space not less than 1 700 mm in diameter...not less than 1 500 mm long...clear floor space not less than 900 mm wide adjacent to its entire length

requirements.label: Bathtub Grab Bar Requirements
requirements.description: Accessible bathtubs must have three grab bars, each at least 1200 mm long: two vertical at each end (80–120 mm from edge, 180–280 mm above rim), one horizontal along the length (180–280 mm above rim).
requirements.evidence: three grab bars...not less than 1 200 mm long...two...located vertically at each end...80 mm to 120 mm from the outside edge...180 mm to 280 mm above the bathtub rim...one...horizontally along the length...180 mm to 280 mm above the bathtub rim

requirements.label: Bathtub Slip-Resistant Surface
requirements.description: Accessible bathtubs must have a slip-resistant bottom surface.
requirements.evidence: have a slip-resistant bottom surface

requirements.label: Bathtub Hand-Held Shower Head
requirements.description: Bathtubs must be equipped with a hand-held shower head with at least 1800 mm of flexible hose, usable at 1200 mm and 2030 mm heights.
requirements.evidence: hand-held shower head with not less than 1 800 mm of flexible hose that can be used in a fixed position at a height of 1 200 mm and 2 030 mm

requirements.label: Barrier-Free Telephone Requirements
requirements.description: Barrier-free telephones must be adjacent to a clear floor space at least 1350 mm by 800 mm, with operable parts no more than 1200 mm above the floor.
requirements.evidence: telephone...adjacent to...clear floor space not less than 1 350 mm by 800 mm...operable parts are not more than 1 200 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Public Telephone Shelf Requirements
requirements.description: Public telephone shelves must be at least 305 mm deep, with a clear space at least 250 mm wide and no obstruction within 250 mm above the surface, and a section max 865 mm high.
requirements.evidence: shelves...not less than 305 mm deep...clear space not less than 250 mm wide having no obstruction within 250 mm above the surface...section...not more than 865 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Dimensions and Marking
requirements.description: Accessible parking stalls must be 2.4 m wide with an adjacent 2.4 m wide access aisle, firm, slip-resistant, level, and clearly marked with signage and pavement symbol.
requirements.evidence: designed as a 2.4 m wide parking stall adjacent to a 2.4 m wide access aisle...firm, slip-resistant and level surface...clearly marked and identified by...sign...and...International Symbol of Access painted on the pavement

requirements.label: Assistive Listening System Coverage
requirements.description: Assistive listening systems must encompass the entire seating area and provide clear communication for information, goods, and services.
requirements.evidence: Assistive listening systems...shall encompass the entire seating area...provide for the clear communication required for the exchange of information, goods and services.

requirements.label: Adult Changing Facility
requirements.description: Adult change tables must have a minimum clear floor space of 900 by 1830 mm in front, and be height adjustable from 450–500 mm (low) to 850–900 mm (high).
requirements.evidence: minimum clear floor space 900 by 1830 mm in front of a change bench...surface height...450 and 500 mm at the low range to...850 and 900 mm at the high range

requirements.label: Accessible Routes
requirements.description: A barrier-free path of travel must connect barrier-free entrances, parking, passenger-loading zones, and public thoroughfares for adaptable dwelling units.
requirements.evidence: A barrier-free path of travel that complies with Subsection 3.8.3. shall be provided between a barrier-free entrance... and... parking area... passenger-loading zone... public thoroughfare.

requirements.label: Exterior Building Entrances
requirements.description: At least one entrance serving an adaptable dwelling unit must be barrier-free.
requirements.evidence: At least one entrance serving an adaptable dwelling unit shall be barrier-free.

requirements.label: Door Minimum Width
requirements.description: Every doorway within adaptable dwelling units must have a clear width not less than 850 mm; doorways to physician clinics/offices and exam/treatment rooms must be at least 915 mm.
requirements.evidence: Every doorway within the dwelling unit shall have a clear width not less than 850 mm... Every doorway... to a physician clinic or office shall have a clear width not less than 915 mm...

requirements.label: Accessible Reception and Counters
requirements.description: Main waiting areas in physician clinics must allow a wheelchair turning space of at least 1700 mm diameter.
requirements.evidence: The main waiting area shall be designed to allow a person using a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1 700 mm in diameter.

requirements.label: Automatic Door Operation
requirements.description: Doors in a barrier-free path to physician clinics/offices must be equipped with a power door operator.
requirements.evidence: Every door... shall be equipped with a power door operator that complies with Subsection 3.8.3.

requirements.label: Mobility Device Space in Meeting Rooms
requirements.description: Examination and treatment rooms must allow a wheelchair turning space of at least 1700 mm diameter.
requirements.evidence: be designed to allow a person using a wheelchair to turn in an open space not less than 1 700 mm in diameter

requirements.label: Accessible Parking Stall Width
requirements.description: Accessible parking stalls must be at least 2.4 m wide, with two adjacent stalls (including access aisle) totaling 4.8 m in width.
requirements.evidence: The 2.4 m wide parking stall is the required minimum width... 2 adjacent stalls to be designated as barrier-free, including the access aisle. 4800 mm

requirements.label: Unobstructed Turning Diameter in Dwellings
requirements.description: Entryways, kitchens, washrooms, laundry rooms, and other areas must have an unobstructed turning diameter of at least 1700 mm.
requirements.evidence: Entryways, kitchens, washrooms, laundry rooms and other areas... shall be designed with an unobstructed turning diameter of not less than 1 700 mm.

requirements.label: Accessible Window Operation
requirements.description: Openable windows must have controls no more than 60 mm above the sill, not require tight grasping, and sill heights between 400 mm and 865 mm above floor.
requirements.evidence: Openable windows shall... be equipped with opening devices located not more than 60 mm above the window sill... sill is not less than 400 mm... not more than 865 mm above the floor

requirements.label: Electrical Receptacle Mounting Height
requirements.description: Electrical receptacles in adaptable dwelling units must be mounted between 400 mm and 1200 mm above the finished floor.
requirements.evidence: Electrical receptacles shall be mounted between 400 mm and 1 200 mm above the finished floor.

requirements.label: Colour Contrast for Frames and Baseboards
requirements.description: Door and window frames and baseboards must be contrasting in colour to doors, walls, and floors.
requirements.evidence: Door and window frames and baseboards shall be contrasting in colour to doors, walls and floors.

requirements.label: Bathroom Fixture Requirements in Adaptable Units
requirements.description: Adaptable dwelling unit bathrooms must provide either a shower or bathtub, with distribution based on even/odd unit count, and allow for future grab bar installation.
requirements.evidence: An adaptable dwelling unit shall be provided with a bathroom containing either a shower or bathtub... designed to allow for the installation of grab bars...

requirements.label: Adjustable Kitchen Counters and Cabinets
requirements.description: Kitchen counters and overhead cabinets in adaptable units must be height-adjustable; counters for sinks/cooktops: 710 mm min height, 800 mm wide, 685 mm high knee clearance.
requirements.evidence: Counters... shall be provided with a means of adjusting their height... not less than 710 mm above the finished floor... clearance beneath... not less than 800 mm wide, 685 mm high...

requirements.label: Assistive Listening Systems in Clinics
requirements.description: An assistive listening system must be provided at the main reception area and in at least one examination or treatment room in physician clinics.
requirements.evidence: An assistive listening system... shall be provided at the main reception area and in at least one physical examination or treatment room.

requirements.label: Alternate Accessible Routes (Stair Alternatives)
requirements.description: Buildings must provide an exterior exit at ground level or a ramp leading to ground level as an accessible route.
requirements.evidence:
d. have an exterior exit at ground level, or
e. have a ramp leading to ground level.

requirements.label: Accessible Balconies for Refuge
requirements.description: Balconies serving as areas of refuge must have direct barrier-free access, be at least 1.5 m deep, and provide 1.5 m2 per non-ambulatory occupant and 0.5 m2 per ambulatory occupant.
requirements.evidence:
a. have direct barrier-free access from the suite or floor area
b. be not less than 1.5 m deep ...
c. provide not less than 1.5 m2 of balcony space for each non-ambulatory occupant and 0.5 m2 for each ambulatory occupant.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Fire alarm systems must provide both audible and visible signal devices in specified locations, including areas for people with hearing impairments and public corridors.
requirements.evidence:
1. Where a fire alarm system is installed, visible signal devices shall be provided in addition to alarm signal devices
a. in buildings or portions thereof intended for use primarily by persons with a hearing impairment, ...
e. in public corridors ...

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Audible signal devices forming part of a fire alarm system shall be installed so that alarm signals are clearly audible throughout the floor area.
requirements.evidence: a. alarm signals are clearly audible throughout the floor area

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Fire alarm audible signal devices in sleeping rooms must emit a low frequency signal.
requirements.evidence: 6. Audible signal devices in sleeping rooms in a building of residential or care occupancy shall emit a low frequency signal.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Fire alarm signal sound pressure level must not exceed 110 dBA in any normally occupied area.
requirements.evidence: 4. The fire alarm signal sound pressure level shall be not more than 110 dBA in any normally occupied area.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Fire alarm audible signal in sleeping rooms must be at least 75 dBA with intervening doors closed.
requirements.evidence: the sound pressure level in a sleeping room from a fire alarm audible signal ... shall be not less than 75 dBA ... when any intervening doors ... are closed.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Fire alarm system’s audible signal device must be at least 10 dBA above ambient noise and not less than 65 dBA with doors closed.
requirements.evidence: the sound pressure level from a fire alarm system’s audible signal device ... shall be not less than 10 dBA above the ambient noise level and not less than 65 dBA when ... doors ... are closed.

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Visible signal devices must be installed so that at least one device is visible throughout the floor area or portion thereof.
requirements.evidence: visible signal devices required by Sentence (1) shall be installed so that the signal from at least one device is visible throughout the floor area

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Visible signal devices in suites of residential occupancy must be located such that at least one device is within the principal living area.
requirements.evidence: at least one device is located within the principal living area.

requirements.label: Visual Signals in Bedrooms and Bathrooms
requirements.description: Visual alarm signals should be placed in bedrooms and bathrooms for safety, as individuals with hearing loss may not perceive alarms from other areas.
requirements.evidence: Sentence 4) should also specify the placement of visual signals in bedrooms and bathrooms for safety.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Wayfinding design must provide clear, logical, and intuitive navigation information using multiple sensory cues (visual, auditory, tactile, olfactory) for independent orientation and movement.
requirements.evidence: Wayfinding design organizes the built environment to provide useful information for navigation by ensuring there is the opportunity to use cognitive and perceptual information to arrive independently at the desired destination.

requirements.label: Braille, Visual, and Audio Cues
requirements.description: Signage should include tactile, Braille, and audible signs to support wayfinding for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Signage, including tactile, Braille, and audible signs

requirements.label: Pattern and Colour Blocking for Key Access
requirements.description: Wayfinding should use textural contrasts, tactile cues, and colour/brightness contrast to support navigation for people with visual disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Textural contrasts and tactile cues, • Colour and brightness contrast

requirements.label: Audible and Visual Alerts
requirements.description: Buildings must have well-planned and well-marked evacuation routes, using both visual and auditory cues for alerts.
requirements.evidence: It is recommended that all buildings have well-planned and well-marked evacuation routes.

requirements.label: Assistive Listening Systems in Public Areas
requirements.description: Install assistive listening devices (ALDs) in all areas where occupancy might be 50 persons or more; public areas requiring PA systems for safety must have magnetic induction loop systems.
requirements.evidence: Assistive Listening Devices (ALD) should be installed in all areas of any building where occupancy might be 50 persons and more. Public areas that require PA systems for reasons of safety should be equipped with a magnetic induction loop systems.

requirements.label: Induction Loop Receiver Ratio
requirements.description: Where an induction loop system is deployed, provide 1 loop receiver for every 50 occupants for those without compatible hearing aids.
requirements.evidence: the building should have 1 loop receiver for every 50 occupants so that people without hearing aids or with hearing aids that do not have a “t coil” will be able to receive the audio

requirements.label: Annual ALD and Loop System Testing
requirements.description: All ALDs and induction loop installations must be checked once per year and a Certificate of Conformity issued in accordance with IEC60118-4 2014 Standard.
requirements.evidence: All ALDs should be checked once per year to ensure proper operation. Once per year each loop installation should be checked and a Certificate of Conformity shall be issued in accordance with the Standard.

requirements.label: Induction Loop System Installation Standard
requirements.description: Installation of induction loop systems must comply with IEC60118-4 2014 Standard, and installer must supply a Certificate of Conformity.
requirements.evidence: Installation of induction loop shall comply with the IEC60118-4 2014 Standard, and a Certificate of Conformity should be issued to ensure proper operation; the installer should supply a Certificate of Conformity.

requirements.label: Counter Loop Systems in Health Care Locations
requirements.description: Health care locations manned by staff must be equipped with a magnetic counter loop system and display the 't coil' symbol.
requirements.evidence: Heath Care locations manned by staff should be equipped with a magnetic counter loop system and the “t coil” symbol should be displayed.

requirements.label: Clear Wayfinding Signage
requirements.description: Provide clear, simple, and concise signage with both text and pictograms to support wayfinding for persons with neurodiversity and cognitive disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Wording on signs should be simple and concise...a combination of both text and pictograms on signage would be helpful.

requirements.label: Pattern and Colour Blocking for Key Access
requirements.description: Provide visual pathways and signage to indicate how to find quiet spaces with reduced visual and auditory stimulation.
requirements.evidence: Provide visual pathways to indicate how to find quiet spaces with reduced visual and auditory stimulation.

requirements.label: Quiet Space Availability
requirements.description: Ensure sufficient space and quiet space options are provided, including a small table for communication devices, to accommodate persons with neurodiversity and communication/cognitive disorders.
requirements.evidence: Ensure sufficient space and quiet space options are provided to accommodate all persons, as well as a small table for a communication device.

requirements.label: Display Communication Access Symbols
requirements.description: Display Communication Access and Hearing Access symbols and strategies at reception desks and other areas where staff are trained to facilitate conversations with persons with communication and/or cognitive disabilities.
requirements.evidence: Communication Access and Hearing Access symbols and strategies should be displayed at reception desks and other areas where staff have been trained...

requirements.label: Display Communication Strategies
requirements.description: Display simple strategies in public and staff areas to guide interactions with people who have cognitive, speech and language or hearing difficulties.
requirements.evidence: Display simple strategies in public and staff areas to guide interactions with people who have cognitive, speech and language or hearing difficulties.
